title: Ring Strategy
lede: Control how a group's online members are rung, how long the group rings before falling back to AI, and a few call-handling behaviors like force auto-answer. These settings apply to inbound calls, AI transfers, and queues alike.

steps.title: Open the Ring strategy tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center → Groups and select a group.
  2. Open the Ring strategy tab.

images.caption: Ring strategy: how members are rung, the AI fallback, and call handling.
Ring strategy: how members are rung, the AI fallback, and call handling.

steps.title: Choose how the group is rung
steps.content:
Under When ringing the group, pick a strategy:

  • Ring all at once — every online member rings; first to answer wins (default).
  • Round-robin — one member at a time, rotating between members.
  • Linear — one at a time, always starting from the top of the member list.
  • Longest idle — ring whoever has waited longest since their last call.

For the one-at-a-time strategies, set Ring each member for (seconds) before the call advances.

steps.title: Set how long before routing to AI
steps.content: Set Ring the group for (seconds) before routing to AI. This is the total time the group is rung. If no member answers within it, the call follows the When no one answers option: route to the number's assigned AI agent, route to a different AI agent, or hang up.

steps.title: Set call-handling behaviors
steps.content:
In the Call handling card, toggle:

  • Force auto-answer — every online member auto-connects group calls without clicking Accept; the first to win takes it.
  • Force auto-open lead/contact — each member's screen opens the caller's lead/contact record as the call rings.
  • Disable auto-leave when the caller hangs up — keep the member on the line after the caller hangs up (special cases only).
  • Hold callers in a queue when no agent is free — on by default; see Queue Settings.

Click Save when finished.

metaDescription: Configure how a Contact Center group rings in iMash — ring all at once or sequentially, seconds before AI fallback, force auto-answer, and auto-open lead/contact.
